# Avorax Anti-Virus Status

## Current Phase

Product-hardening sprint for Avorax Anti-Virus. MSI/EXE installers remain first-install, repair, recovery, offline, and manual-install paths only. Normal app updates target signed `.aup` packages applied by Avorax Update Service.

## Current Commit

- Current checkpoint commit: this working tree; run `git log -1 --oneline` after commit for the exact SHA.
- Current public release tag: `v0.1.15-beta.3`, published as a prerelease on 2026-07-20 with independently verified checksums. Checkpoint 2178 is source hardening and does not create a new release tag.

## Latest Checkpoint Evidence - 2026-07-20

- Current explicit driver-activation boundary pass: checkpoint 2178 removes the
  elevated minifilter custom action from ordinary Windows MSI/EXE installation.
  Candidate driver files remain inert package content; the separate helper
  requires `-ConfirmDriverInstall`, never imports bundled certificates into
  `Root` or `TrustedPublisher`, and never enables TESTSIGNING. Local evidence
  passes: `616` source contracts, `22` packaging tests with `3` expected Windows
  symlink skips, parser and no-confirm helper checks, product/no-malware safety
  gates, an actual Avorax MSI database/extraction/lifecycle pass, and rejection
  of a real cached MSI containing a `CustomAction` table before extraction.
  Fresh native package CI remains pending before merge. Production driver signing
  and disposable elevated-host driver E2E remain blocked.

- Current Ultracode portable-beta release preparation pass: checkpoint 2154 adds a prominent English beta safety disclaimer to the root and packaged READMEs and creates `docs\releases\avorax-portable-beta-0.1.0-beta.1.md`. The disclaimer states that this experimental manual scanner must not be the only antivirus, may miss advanced/novel/targeted/polymorphic/fileless/kernel-level/large-scale malware and ransomware, and requires Defender or another supported antivirus to remain enabled. The Windows portable bundle was rebuilt and re-extracted after the copy change; build passed in `5.715s`, archive smoke in `4.599s`, all four adversarial ZIP cases were rejected, and cleanup passed. The current artifact is `dist\Avorax-Portable-Beta-0.1.0-beta.1.zip`, `5,886,708` bytes, SHA-256 `a80155373a869576dad6d015c21221a18815bf3318a253a11c19477af128240b`. App-wide accessibility regression coverage also now checks all ten shell routes plus onboarding for labeled tap targets, Android target sizes, and 200% mobile text; all `21` tests pass after responsive header/status/checklist/action/metric/dropdown fixes and live-region notification semantics. GitHub publication is limited to this verified Windows ZIP: no MSI, standalone launcher EXE, or Linux binary is claimed or published without a real platform build and runtime verification.

- Current Ultracode portable small-threat beta pass: checkpoint 2153 adds a non-installing `Avorax Portable Scanner Beta` bundle for the currently verified release local core while the Windows GUI/MSI host prerequisites remain unavailable. `tools\windows\avorax-portable-beta.ps1` exposes status, Quick/Full/File/Folder scans, a bounded explicit-folder watch, quarantine list/restore/delete/rescan, and allowlist list/add/remove through the existing fail-visible wrappers; runtime data and reports stay under `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Avorax\PortableBeta` unless an explicit local data root is selected. `tools\windows\build-avorax-portable-beta.ps1` packages only the canonical core, runtime engine signature/rule/model/trust assets, security helpers, wrappers, documentation, and launch commands, then requires ready status plus a harmless scan/quarantine/list/restore/delete lifecycle before hashing every file and producing the ZIP. `tools\testing\run-avorax-portable-beta-smoke.ps1` safely re-extracts and verifies the ZIP with entry/count/size/total/ratio/path/duplicate limits, validates every manifest hash, reruns status and lifecycle from the extracted copy, and rejects parent traversal, case-insensitive duplicate names, excessive compression ratio, and a tampered manifest hash. The artifact is `dist\Avorax-Portable-Beta-0.1.0-beta.1.zip`, SHA-256 `b5e75c2404a486639b79407b9eca2afe90258e9c076a7416679723057af1af02`; build evidence records `13` signatures, `9` rules, native self-test success, local stdio/no-network operation, `39` manifested files, and all lifecycle postconditions. PowerShell parser checks, extracted-archive smoke, and Python source-contracts (`582`) passed. Evidence is recorded in `.workflow\ultracode\avorax-hardening\results\2153-portable-small-threat-beta.md`. This is an unsigned portable manual/finite user-mode scanner, not an installed GUI, service, persistent monitor, Defender replacement, driver, or pre-execution blocker.

- Current Ultracode installed-core lifecycle pass: checkpoint 2152 adds `tools\windows\avorax-installed-core-lifecycle-probe.ps1`, which drives the canonical `zentor_local_core.exe` through the real local-scan and quarantine wrappers using only isolated harmless ASCII exact-hash fixtures. The probe fails unless the first source is removed into a verified `.avoraxq` payload, list returns the same record, confirmed restore reproduces the original SHA-256 and removes the payload, and a second confirmed quarantine/delete leaves both source and payload absent. Temporary data is removed only after a strict temp-root name/parent/reparse check. The installed smoke now runs this probe only after layout, service, manifest, and structured-health checks have passed, validates the core hash and lifecycle postconditions, and writes `ProgramData\Avorax\reports\installed_core_lifecycle_report.json`; installer stage/release gates require the probe, wrappers, and shared security helpers. The central verifier records and independently schema-validates `generated_reports.installed_core_lifecycle`. Focused lifecycle runtime, PowerShell parser checks, Python source-contracts (`581`), a reconstructed full-report validator pass (`216` steps), and tampered-evidence rejection passed. The full small-threat MVP verifier/report-validator passed all `216` report steps in `1092.7s` for `.workflow\ultracode\avorax-hardening\results\2152-small-threat-mvp-installed-lifecycle-report.json`; `Installed core lifecycle probe release-binary smoke` passed in `3.8s` and final report validation in `2.2s`. A negative fixture with `operations.restore.payload_removed=false` was rejected; output is captured in `.workflow\ultracode\avorax-hardening\results\2152-tampered-installed-lifecycle-validator.txt`. Evidence is recorded in `.workflow\ultracode\avorax-hardening\results\2152-installed-core-lifecycle.md`. This verifies the release executable plus wrapper stdio lifecycle and installed-smoke wiring, not an installed service transport, packaged UI click-through, or pre-execution blocking. Actual MSI/EXE build and installed E2E remain blocked by no installed .NET SDK, missing Visual Studio Desktop C++ components, and unavailable Windows symlink support.

- Current Ultracode structured installed core-health pass: checkpoint 2151 removes the installed smoke test's weak stdout substring acceptance (`"ok":true`) and replaces it with `tools\windows\avorax-core-health-probe.ps1`, a bounded structured JSON probe shared by installed verification and a release-binary regression smoke. The parser requires exactly one non-progress JSON response, `ok=true`, the complete health field set, typed non-negative signature/rule counts, `ipc=stdio`, and `network_exposed=false`; malformed JSON, missing fields, multiple responses, explicit failure, non-stdio IPC, network exposure, and non-zero exit all fail visibly. `avorax-installed-smoke-test.ps1` now probes the canonical installed `zentor_local_core.exe`, requires its SHA-256 to match the `avorax_core_service.exe` alias, rejects stderr, and requires available/ready engine state, positive signature/rule counts, and a passing native self-test. `avorax-installer-stage-test.ps1` requires the shared probe in the staged payload. Focused parser/runtime smoke, PowerShell parser checks, and Python source-contracts (`580`) passed; `.workflow\ultracode\avorax-hardening\results\installed-core-health-probe.json` records seven rejected parser cases plus a real release-binary local health response. The full small-threat MVP verifier passed all `215` report steps in `1027.4s` for `.workflow\ultracode\avorax-hardening\results\2151-small-threat-mvp-structured-installed-health-report.json`, including `Installed smoke structured core-health probe tests` (`2.1s`), and the post-report validator passed in `1.7s`. Negative evidence passed: `.workflow\ultracode\avorax-hardening\results\2151-small-threat-mvp-missing-structured-health-step-report.json` failed with `passed full-suite report is missing required step: Installed smoke structured core-health probe tests`; output is captured in `.workflow\ultracode\avorax-hardening\results\2151-structured-installed-health-negative-validator.txt`. Evidence is recorded in `.workflow\ultracode\avorax-hardening\results\2151-structured-installed-health.md`. This removes fake-success acceptance and strengthens the future installed gate, but actual installed service/UI E2E remains blocked on this host by the missing .NET SDK, Visual Studio Desktop C++ components, and Windows symlink support; no installed-runtime claim is made from the release-binary probe.

- Current Ultracode status/allowlist/cancel path/report guard release-binary pass: checkpoint 2150 extends `tools\testing\run-avorax-status-wrapper-smoke.ps1`, `run-avorax-allowlist-wrapper-smoke.ps1`, and `run-avorax-cancel-scan-wrapper-smoke.ps1` with fail-closed negative-input evidence over `target\release\zentor_local_core.exe`. The smokes write `status-wrapper-path-guards.json` (`2` cases), `allowlist-wrapper-path-guards.json` (`3` cases), and `cancel-scan-wrapper-path-guards.json` (`3` cases). They prove missing engine roots, unconfirmed allowlist add/remove actions, missing or mutually exclusive cancel data-root selection, and absolute report paths outside the repository fail visibly. All eight rejected cases record `report_written=false`; the outside-report cancel case also records `cancel_token_written=false`, proving local-core cancellation was not invoked after report-path rejection. The verifier now requires separate status, allowlist, and cancel `path/report guard smoke` scopes, and the validator rejects full-suite reports missing any one of them. PowerShell parser checks, all three focused wrapper smokes, and Python source-contracts (`580`) passed. The full small-threat MVP verifier passed all `214` report steps in `1006.1s` for `.workflow\ultracode\avorax-hardening\results\2150-small-threat-mvp-user-flow-path-guards-report.json`; `Cancel scan wrapper release-binary smoke` passed in `2.4s`, `Allowlist wrapper release-binary smoke` in `6.3s`, `Status wrapper release-binary smoke` in `2.7s`, and the post-report full-suite validator passed in `1.6s`. Evidence is recorded in `.workflow\ultracode\avorax-hardening\results\2150-user-flow-path-guards.md`. This is safe release-binary user-flow boundary proof only; installed packaged UI click-through, installed local-core/service E2E, persistent background monitoring, scheduled startup, signed-driver/pre-execution blocking, production false-positive rates, release-host SBOM/license output, and release packaging remain partial or blocked.

- Current Ultracode timeout process-tree cleanup pass: checkpoint 2148 hardens Windows timeout cleanup for Flutter app-detection, platform PowerShell, local-core IPC, Guard self-test, cancel-IPC, and elevated PowerShell helper processes. Timed-out helpers now attempt bounded Windows process-tree cleanup using the checked `%SystemRoot%\System32\taskkill.exe` with `/PID <pid> /T /F` for the Avorax-spawned child before falling back to single-process kill/reap diagnostics. Runtime tests now assert the injected hung Dart fixture process exits after timeout, and source contracts pin the `taskkill.exe` path resolution, `/T`/`/F` arguments, verifier step, validator scope, and no-leftover-process evidence. The finite watch-scan wrapper smoke was also race-hardened by using `DurationSeconds=8` and waiting `2500ms` before writing the harmless fixture; focused smoke counters now record `initial_files_observed=0`, `events_observed=1`, `files_scanned=1`, and expected detect/quarantine results. Evidence is recorded in `.workflow\ultracode\avorax-hardening\results\2148-timeout-process-tree-cleanup.md`. - Current Ultracode shell notification priority pass: checkpoint 2144 hardens `ZentorShell` so in-app security notifications are selected by severity priority instead of being hidden by a later informational scan event. The shell now scans the recent local-event window, ranks `error` above `warning` above informational events, and uses newest-event ordering only as a tie-breaker within the same priority. Added widget coverage proves an older `threat_detected` warning remains visible over a newer `scan_completed` info event, and newest warning selection still works when priorities match. The small-threat verifier now runs `Flutter shell notification priority tests`; the report validator rejects passed no-skip-Flutter reports missing that step or the `security-prioritized shell notification summaries` scope. Verification passed locally: Dart format (`0 changed` after formatting), Flutter analyze (`No issues found`), UI inventory gate (`61` source-accounted controls), focused `flutter test test\navigation_accessibility_test.dart --plain-name "shell notification"` (`3` tests), focused `flutter test test\app_visual_policy_test.dart --plain-name "shell exposes in-app notifications"` (`1` test), PowerShell parser checks, and Python source-contracts (`580`). Full small-threat MVP verification passed with `213` report steps in `606.9s` for `.workflow\ultracode\avorax-hardening\results\2144-small-threat-mvp-shell-notification-priority-report.json`, including `Flutter shell notification priority tests` (`4s`); the post-write `Small-threat MVP report validator -RequireFullSuite` passed (`1.3s`). Evidence is recorded in `.workflow\ultracode\avorax-hardening\results\2144-shell-notification-priority.md`. This is in-app local-event notification proof only; Windows toast delivery, installed packaged UI click-through, persistent monitoring, signed-driver/pre-execution blocking, and live-malware behavior remain partial, blocked, or not claimed.

- Current Ultracode manual quarantine UI/controller/IPC pass: checkpoint 2143 adds a confirmation-gated `Quarantine file` action to the Flutter Quarantine screen, backed by `ZentorController.quarantineSelectedFile(confirmed: true)` and local-core `quarantine_file` IPC. The flow refuses non-desktop hosts, scan/configuration/update/quarantine busy states, and missing confirmation before opening the OS file picker; picker cancel clears busy state, picker errors are surfaced, and confirmed selections send bounded manual labels (`Manual quarantine`, `avorax-ui-manual-quarantine`) to local-core before refreshing quarantine state. Updated widget/controller/IPC tests prove cancel does not pick a file, confirm picks exactly once, busy/update states block selection, failures are normalized, and the IPC payload includes explicit `path`, `threat_name`, and `engine` fields. The small-threat verifier now runs and the validator now requires `Flutter manual quarantine IPC tests` in addition to quarantine controller/screen tests and the existing `manual quarantine file-picker UI/controller/IPC guards` scope. The full small-threat MVP verifier now requires `Flutter watch-poll IPC diagnostics tests` and `Flutter watch-poll loop controller tests`; both passed inside the 2114 full run. - Current Ultracode native Authenticode target-path invocation pass: checkpoint 2082 fixes the Windows Microsoft publisher-trust probe so the checked `System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe` helper no longer appends the inspected file path as a positional argument after `-EncodedCommand`, which fails on this host with `Cannot process command because a command is already specified with -Command or -EncodedCommand.` ANE now passes the candidate path through the process-only `AVORAX_AUTHENTICODE_TARGET_PATH` environment variable and the encoded helper reads it with `[Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable(..., 'Process')` before calling `Get-AuthenticodeSignature -LiteralPath $target`. The bounded stdout/stderr runner, checked local PowerShell path, UTF-16LE `-EncodedCommand`, no raw `-Command`, and fail-visible diagnostics are preserved. Native runtime proof now verifies an unsigned temporary `.exe` returns `false` instead of an Authenticode command failure, native trust tests passed (`56`), local-core Quick Scan reports passed (`32`) with the EML category expectation aligned to either suspicious downloader or suspicious script review evidence, and the full small-threat MVP verifier plus report validator passed with `148` steps in `344.9s` for `.workflow\ultracode\avorax-hardening\results\2082-small-threat-mvp-full-report.json`. - Current Ultracode Quick Scan OOXML relationship limit-evidence pass: checkpoint 2067 makes oversized and unsupported-compression OOXML `.rels` bodies fail-visible instead of silently looking clean. ANE now returns limit/unsupported evidence when a stored `.rels` body exceeds `64 KiB`, a deflated `.rels` body exceeds the compressed/declared/decoded `64 KiB` bounds, or a relationship entry uses an unsupported ZIP compression method. Local-core Quick Scan proves an inert `unsupported-compression-invoice.docm` fixture whose unsupported-compression relationship body contains plaintext-looking remote-script XML remains clean and unquarantined because unsupported relationship bytes are not trusted as OOXML evidence. - Current Ultracode Quick Scan encrypted OOXML relationship trust-boundary pass: checkpoint 2066 prevents encrypted ZIP relationship entries from becoming trusted OOXML `.rels` evidence. ANE now reads the ZIP general-purpose encrypted bit from local headers and central-directory entries, refuses to inspect encrypted `.rels` bodies, marks the archive as limit/unsupported evidence instead of clean success, and requires the encrypted bit to match between central-directory metadata and the referenced local header before central-directory body reads. Local-core Quick Scan proves an inert `encrypted-invoice.docm` fixture whose encrypted relationship body contains plaintext-looking remote-script XML remains clean and unquarantined because encrypted relationship bytes are not trusted. - Current Ultracode Quick Scan OOXML data-descriptor relationship runtime coverage pass: checkpoint 2065 adds bounded central-directory parsing to ANE's ZIP analyzer so macro-enabled OOXML packages that store zero local-header sizes and put the real sizes in the central directory can still surface small `.rels` relationship evidence. The central-directory path is bounded to `256 KiB`, rejects ZIP64/multidisk sentinels as limit evidence, requires the central entry's local-header name, method, and data-descriptor bit to match before reading a body, and still applies the existing stored/deflated 64 KiB relationship-body limits without extracting entries to disk.   - 1414: hardened in-app update local feed trust so `file:` feed URIs with an authority/host are rejected before path conversion, preventing UNC-like feed authorities from being treated as local.

  - 1421: hardened update feed product matching so the `product` field is parsed through the required string boundary before the Avorax product comparison.
  - 1422: hardened update release-notes parsing so free-form release notes reject NUL and unsafe control characters while still allowing tabs and line breaks.
  - 1423: hardened GitHub update asset-name trust so release asset path names must pass the safe update asset-name boundary before fallback or redirect matching.
  - 1424: hardened GitHub update redirect Location parsing so bounded Location header text rejects raw control/NUL characters before URI resolution.
  - 1425: hardened GitHub release-asset redirect path trust so the final `release-assets.githubusercontent.com` hop requires a non-empty, dot-segment-free GitHub production release asset path with at least three segments.
  - 1426: hardened GitHub update redirect Location raw-header validation so control/NUL and length checks run on the untrimmed Location value before whitespace trimming or URI resolution.
  - 1427: hardened update `Content-Length` parsing so present header values are length-checked and rejected for raw control/NUL characters before whitespace trimming or numeric parsing.
  - 1428: hardened signed `.aup` archive validation so only `manifest.json`, `manifest.sig`, and safe `payload/...` entries are accepted before manifest reads or payload limit scans.
  - 1429: hardened signed manifest scalar validation so versions, IDs, release dates, release notes URLs, migration steps, and optional package hashes reject raw control characters and surrounding whitespace before trimmed parsing.
  - 1430: hardened signed manifest payload-hash values so raw control characters and surrounding whitespace are rejected before `.aup` archive open or payload hash comparison.
  - 1431: hardened signed manifest payload-hash path keys so raw control characters and surrounding whitespace are rejected before path normalization or `.aup` archive open.
  - 1432: hardened normal `.aup` tooling payload policy so signed packages and the package builder reject `tools/` payload roots unless a separate explicit tooling workflow is implemented.
  - 1433: removed normal update applier activation for `tools/` payload sections so rejected tooling payloads have no install-copy route in the normal `.aup` apply path.
  - 1434: disabled normal `.aup` migration payloads and migration steps until a separate explicit migration workflow exists, removing verifier, builder, and applier half-support.
  - 1435: tightened normal `.aup` service payload policy so only direct `avorax_core_service.exe` and `avorax_guard_service.exe` service payloads are supported.
  - 1436: tightened normal `.aup` engine payload policy so only files under `engine/signatures`, `engine/rules`, `engine/ml`, and `engine/trust` are accepted by verifier and builder.
  - 1437: tightened normal `.aup` engine activation so the applier copies only allowlisted engine runtime subdirectories instead of activating `staging\engine` wholesale.
  - 1438: tightened normal `.aup` service activation so the applier stages only allowlisted Core and Guard service binaries instead of activating `staging\services` wholesale.
  - 1439: tightened normal `.aup` docs payload policy and activation so verifier, builder, and applier accept and stage only Markdown files under `docs`.
  - 1440: tightened normal `.aup` app payload policy and activation so `payload/app` cannot target restricted install-root surfaces or managed service/updater executables.
  - 1441: tightened normal `.aup` app component evidence so the builder declares `components.app` when any staged app payload file exists, not only when `Avorax.exe` is present.
  - 1442: tightened normal `.aup` engine component evidence so builder component flags require runtime files and empty engine runtime directories fail visibly.
  - 1443: tightened normal `.aup` docs component evidence so the builder declares `components.docs` only when staged docs payload files exist.
  - 1444: tightened normal `.aup` service component evidence so the builder derives Core and Guard service component flags from explicit staged service payload file checks.
  - 1445: tightened normal `.aup` app top-level file staging so each source file is revalidated through `Require-File` at copy time.
  - 1446: tightened normal `.aup` app directory staging so each app directory source and destination are revalidated before recursive copy.
  - 1447: tightened normal `.aup` engine component staging so each engine runtime component source and destination are revalidated before recursive copy.
  - 1448: tightened normal `.aup` top-level app and service file staging so source files and destination paths are revalidated before copy.
  - 1449: tightened normal `.aup` docs file staging so Markdown source files and destination paths are revalidated before copy.
  - 1450: tightened update-package artifact activation so package, temporary zip, and backup paths are revalidated before creation/activation and the final package file is revalidated before hashing.
  - 1451: tightened update-package zipping so each work-tree source file is revalidated through `Require-File` immediately before `CreateEntryFromFile`.
  - 1452: tightened update-package zipping so only `manifest.json`, `manifest.sig`, and `payload/...` work-tree entries can be written into a normal `.aup` archive.
  - 1453: tightened update-package signing so the manifest signature output path is revalidated before invoking the signer and the produced signature is revalidated as a regular file.

  - 1458: tightened update-package payload hashing so each payload file is revalidated through `Require-File` immediately before SHA-256 hashing.
  - 1459: tightened update atomic JSON/feed writes so generated temporary and backup paths are revalidated before write/activation and stale random-name collisions are cleaned through checked regular-file removal.

  - 1461: tightened update package activation so final `.aup` `File.Replace` and `File.Move` use the checked temporary package returned by `Require-File`.

  - 1466: tightened update payload hashing so hash enumeration uses the shared no-reparse payload-file helper.

  - 1468: tightened update package zipping so the work tree is revalidated for reparse entries immediately before archive enumeration.

  - 1471: tightened docs payload staging so the docs source tree is revalidated for reparse entries immediately before Markdown file copy staging.

## Completed Items In This Checkpoint

- Checkpoint 1768 hardens local quarantine payload cleanup so restore post-status cleanup and delete payload removal revalidate the payload as a non-following regular quarantine file immediately before `remove_file`. Focused local-core `quarantine` tests pass (`87`), full local-core tests pass (`419`), `cargo fmt --all -- --check` passes, source-contracts pass (`481`), no-malware/product-copy gates pass, and Python contract scripts compile.
- Checkpoint 1769 hardens signed `.aup` payload extraction activation so the target parent chain is revalidated after the non-following target-absence check and immediately before final `rename`. The focused activation regression passes, full `avorax_update_service` tests pass (`176`), and `cargo fmt --all -- --check` passes.
- Checkpoint 1770 hardens Flutter update-service timeout diagnostics so timed-out updater subprocesses report kill request status plus a bounded post-kill exit observation instead of stopping at "termination requested." `update_service_test.dart` passes (`108`), `flutter analyze` reports no issues, source-contracts pass (`481`), no-malware/product-copy gates pass, and Python contract scripts compile.
- Checkpoint 1771 hardens Flutter local-core subprocess timeout diagnostics so scan IPC, Guard self-test, cancel IPC, and elevated PowerShell paths report kill request status plus bounded post-kill exit observation. `local_core_ipc_diagnostics_test.dart` passes (`59`), `flutter analyze` reports no issues, source-contracts pass (`481`), no-malware/product-copy gates pass, and Python contract scripts compile.
- Checkpoint 1772 hardens Flutter platform-info PowerShell and app-detection process-list timeout diagnostics so both paths report kill request status plus bounded post-kill exit observation and avoid hanging while collecting timed-out streams. `platform_info_service_test.dart`/`app_detector_test.dart` pass, `flutter analyze` reports no issues, source-contracts pass (`481`), no-malware/product-copy gates pass, and Python contract scripts compile.
- Checkpoint 1773 adds benign hung-Dart-process runtime fixtures for the Flutter platform-info PowerShell and app-detection process-list timeout paths, using injectable process starters and short test-only timeouts while preserving production command resolution and timeout defaults. `platform_info_service_test.dart` passes (`19`), `app_detector_test.dart` passes (`9`), `flutter analyze` reports no issues, source-contracts pass (`481`), no-malware/product-copy gates pass, and Python contract scripts compile.
- Checkpoint 1774 adds benign hung-Dart-process runtime fixtures for Flutter local-core Guard self-test, cancel IPC, and elevated PowerShell timeout cleanup. Local-core subprocess helpers now support test-only process starters/timeouts while preserving production defaults, and elevated PowerShell timeout diagnostics bound post-timeout stdout/stderr collection. `local_core_ipc_diagnostics_test.dart` passes (`62`), `flutter analyze` reports no issues, source-contracts pass (`481`), no-malware/product-copy gates pass, and Python contract scripts compile.
- Checkpoint 1775 runtime-verifies Flutter cached update-package hash-input size bounds: `verifyDownloadedPackage` rejects an oversized managed-cache `.aup` before SHA-256 streaming/updater use, using a positive test-only package-size limit while preserving the production 512 MiB default. `update_service_test.dart` passes (`109`), `flutter analyze` reports no issues, source-contracts pass (`481`), no-malware/product-copy gates pass, and Python contract scripts compile.
- Checkpoint 1776 runtime-verifies Flutter selected-file hash post-stat and actual-read guards: `HashService` rejects a selected path that changes after stat and rejects a file that grows over a test-only hash limit while streaming, while preserving the production 512 MiB default. `hash_service_test.dart` passes (`6`), `flutter analyze` reports no issues, source-contracts pass (`481`), and no-malware/product-copy gates pass.
- Checkpoint 1777 runtime-verifies Flutter scan metric no-evidence labels: Scan screen widgets show `No report` for report-backed metrics without `lastScanReport` and `Pending` for live progress facts without a progress snapshot, with explicit anti-`0` expectations. `scan_screen_test.dart` passes (`2`), `flutter analyze` reports no issues, source-contracts pass (`481`), and no-malware/product-copy gates pass.
- Checkpoint 1778 runtime-verifies Flutter cloud health streamed-response limits: the API client rejects oversized streamed health responses before buffering even without `Content-Length`, and stalled cloud response streams fail through bounded offline diagnostics using a short test-only timeout. `api_client_test.dart` passes (`40`), `flutter analyze` reports no issues, source-contracts pass (`481`), and no-malware/product-copy gates pass.

- Checkpoint 1870 hardens the Windows release prerequisite report boundary: `avorax-release-prereq-check.ps1` now refuses `-ReportPath` values that resolve outside the repository before writing evidence. Current host-only release blockers remain exactly three: no .NET SDK inventory, unavailable symlink support/Developer Mode, and missing Visual Studio Desktop C++ components.
- Checkpoint 1871 hardens installer-stage and installed-smoke release-manifest path validation so manifest entries with leading/trailing/doubled separators are rejected after slash normalization, closing an alias gap before duplicate-path and installed/staged file hash evidence is trusted. Evidence is recorded in `.workflow\ultracode\avorax-hardening\results\1871-stage-smoke-manifest-path-boundary.md`. Full packaged installer and installed-service E2E remain partial.

A temporary extracted-helper negative fixture fails with the new driver-INF path error, source-contracts pass (`499`), and no-malware/product-copy gates pass. - Checkpoint 1892 hardens generated elevated driver-install helper root evidence: the `avorax-install-driver.ps1` generated by `installer\windows\build-msi.ps1` now requires the installed Avorax driver package directory to exist as a non-reparse directory before deriving the default `ZentorAvFilter.inf`; missing installed driver package evidence fails with an explicit blocker instead of a raw `Get-Item` error or blind path join. A temporary extracted-helper negative fixture fails with the new missing-driver-package-root error before report/system-tool/driver work, source-contracts pass (`499`), and no-malware/product-copy gates pass. Evidence is recorded in `.workflow\ultracode\avorax-hardening\results\1892-generated-driver-install-root-exists.md`. Real driver install evidence remains blocked by signed-driver/host prerequisites.
- Checkpoint 1767 hardens in-app scheduled quick scan activation so timer creation is attempted before config persistence, pending timers are cancelled on failure, and startup timer failures log `scheduled_quick_scan_startup_failed` instead of crashing. A failing timer factory no longer saves an enabled schedule or emits fake success. Focused `offline_scan_test.dart` passes (`103`), `flutter analyze` reports no issues, source-contracts pass (`481`), no-malware/product-copy gates pass, and Python contract scripts compile.
- Checkpoint 1766 surfaces process snapshot evidence on the Protected Apps screen from the latest real local event, with explicit labels for evaluated/suspicious/empty/failed outcomes and bounded details. It does not claim installed polling or pre-execution blocking. Focused Flutter UI/event tests pass (`app_visual_policy_test.dart` + `local_event_test.dart`: `98`), the broader process-snapshot Flutter suite passes (`165`), `flutter analyze` reports no issues, source-contracts pass (`481`), and no-malware/product-copy gates pass.
- Checkpoint 1765 wires the bounded process snapshot path into `ZentorController.unawaitedDetectApps()`: after supported protected-app detection, the controller submits collected `ProcessObservation` rows to Local Core, records `process_snapshot_evaluated` or `process_snapshot_suspicious` evidence with observed/skipped/finding counts and capability/status, and logs `process_snapshot_failed` visibly without breaking app-detection results. Focused Flutter tests pass (`local_event_test.dart`: `40`; combined app-detector/local-core IPC/local-event suite: `107`), `flutter analyze` reports no issues, and source-contracts pass (`481`).
- Checkpoint 1764 extended Flutter AppDetector so the existing checked process-list boundary produces bounded `ProcessObservation` snapshots with PID/image evidence for Local Core process snapshot evaluation. Protected-app detection still derives names from those observations; app-detector tests pass (`8`), local-core IPC diagnostics pass (`59`), and `flutter analyze` reports no issues.
- Checkpoint 1763 added Flutter `LocalCoreClient.evaluateProcessSnapshot()` support, typed process observation/policy/report/finding models, protocol-warning diagnostics, and `userModeSnapshot` health capability parsing. Focused Flutter IPC tests pass (`59`) and `flutter analyze` reports no issues; live process enumeration/UI surfacing remains partial.
- Checkpoint 1762 wired the bounded process-monitor snapshot evaluator into Local Core IPC as `evaluate_process_snapshot` with strict `process_observations`/`process_monitor_policy` schemas. Runtime tests verify suspicious snapshot reporting, exact allowlisting, nested unknown-field rejection, and no active-loop claim; local-core focused IPC tests pass (`3`) and full local-core tests pass (`418`).
- Checkpoint 1761 added a bounded Local Core process-monitor snapshot evaluator with strict observation/policy schemas, normalized allowlists, parent-traversal rejection, bounded text/inventory/findings, and explainable suspicious-process findings for supplied benign metadata. `process_monitor_status` remains `notActive` because no installed polling loop or pre-execution enforcement is claimed; local-core focused tests pass (`5`) and full local-core tests pass (`415`).
- Checkpoint 1760 refreshed the current verification snapshot after local Flutter/Dart, Cargo/rustfmt, and Git became available through explicit Windows paths; `cargo check --workspace --release` passes, Rust release service binaries are present, and release approval remains blocked by no `.NET SDK` inventory, unavailable symlink support, missing Visual Studio Desktop C++ components, missing Windows `Avorax.exe`/installer stage, absent installed service/UI E2E, no `.git` metadata, and no signed-driver/pre-execution validation.
- Added `core/avorax_update_service`, a Rust Windows service and CLI for `--verify` and `--apply` of signed `.aup` packages.
- Added `.aup` manifest validation for Avorax product identity, format version, channel, monotonic version, Ed25519 signature metadata, package hash, per-file payload hashes, and explicit rejection of driver updates.
- Added structured update payload application for `app`, `services`, `engine`, `docs`, `tools`, and `migrations` sections, with rollback snapshot creation before replacement.
- Reworked the Flutter update service so normal updates use `update-feed.json` plus `.aup` packages, verify package SHA-256, call `avorax_update_service --verify`, and elevate `avorax_update_service --apply` instead of launching an EXE/MSI installer.
- Added an Updates screen, sidebar route, update status rows, and app-state plumbing for download, verify, and install progress.
- Added `AVORAX_UPDATE_FEED_URL` and `AVORAX_UPDATE_CHANNEL` build config values.
- Updated Windows MSI packaging to build, include, install, and register `avorax_update_service.exe`, and to create `C:\ProgramData\Avorax\updates\staging`, `rollback`, and `logs`.
- Added `tools/update/avorax-build-update-package.ps1`, which builds a structured `.aup` from the installer stage and refuses unsigned packages or packages missing the Update Service/engine assets.
- Added release-gate checks that reject normal updater code paths referencing `setup.exe`, `.msi`, `msiexec`, or `launchUrl`, and require `.aup` usage.
- Added installed smoke-test and installer-stage checks for the Update Service, update directories, update tools, and ML/native-engine assets.
- Added `docs/in-app-updates.md` and `docs/reports/update-flow-audit.md`.
- Added `packages/avorax_protocol` with shared update manifest models for future client/service protocol alignment.
- Wired the Scan page engine-unavailable recovery actions to real local operations: elevated Avorax Core Service start, explicit service registration/start repair, and install-report opening.
- Added audit logging for install-report open and installation-repair requests, and made scan errors distinguish stopped or missing Core Service states from generic native-engine unavailability.
- Generated `dist\Avorax-AntiVirus-0.2.14-x64.msi` and `dist\Avorax-AntiVirus-0.2.14-x64-setup.exe` from a local Windows toolchain provisioned with Flutter 3.44.0, Rust 1.96.0, .NET SDK 8.0.421, and WiX 6.0.2.
- Generated release installer artifacts for this checkpoint: `dist\Avorax-AntiVirus-0.2.15-x64.msi` and `dist\Avorax-AntiVirus-0.2.15-x64-setup.exe`.
- Patched Windows MSI/EXE packaging so the Avorax Native Engine installed layout (`engine\config`, `engine\signatures`, `engine\rules`, `engine\ml`, and `engine\trust`) is staged into the installer automatically for both direct MSI installs and the EXE bootstrapper.
- Added visible installer proof UI: the MSI uses `WixUI_Minimal` with a license/proof page, and the EXE bootstrapper uses WiX Standard BA visible UI/progress while embedding the MSI.
- Verified the staged Avorax Core Service can load the packaged installed engine layout from `dist\windows-msi\stage\engine`, with native engine status `ready`, 17 native signatures, 11 native rules, development ML loaded, and native self-test passing.
- Preserved native signature category evidence in the risk-fusion path so confirmed hash signatures retain their declared threat category in final verdicts.
- Tightened the installer stage gate so generated WiX validation checks visible product-facing copy, visible installer UI/progress, and native-engine assets without failing on non-visible WiX IDs or technical compatibility paths.
- Created `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `SECURITY_MODEL.md`, `TESTING.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `TODO.md`, and `RUN_LOG.md` for the hardening sprint.
- Hardened Flutter scan orchestration so successful scans clear stale engine errors and cancelled scan progress cannot overwrite cancelled state.
- Reworked Quick Scan target planning into testable platform-specific scopes for Windows/macOS/Linux high-risk locations.
- Hardened local event history loading so corrupt JSON or invalid records cannot crash startup or logs.
- Hardened quarantine restore/delete safety with explicit payload-path validation, corrupt metadata recovery, restore/delete confirmation tests, and duplicate restore-name handling.
- Added UI confirmation dialogs for quarantine restore and permanent delete actions.
- Improved Settings safety with reset confirmation, log-export feedback, and clean developer override disable behavior.
- Polished dashboard/protection/scan states to avoid unsupported claims, remove dead Pause/Keep controls, and show real Core Service status instead of hard-coded running text.
- Added visual policy tests for destructive quarantine confirmation and protection service-state honesty.
- Added best-effort user-mode watcher planning and file-event evaluation in `core/zentor_local_core/src/watcher/mod.rs`, including accessible-path filtering, debounce waiting, stable-file retry, unchanged-file cache suppression, and monitor-only review observations that do not label or block malware.
- Wired `start_watch` to return an honest `userModeBestEffort` watcher state for existing requested paths instead of always returning stopped.
- Improved Flutter `LocalCoreClient` IPC diagnostics for executable-missing, stderr/non-zero exit, malformed JSON, and timeout cases; health summary now exposes IPC failures through `lastError` for recovery UI messaging.
- Wired Flutter protection startup/shutdown to `start_watch` / `stop_watch` for selected existing protected folders, keeps selected app paths in scan/watch paths, and displays `userModeBestEffort` honestly as best-effort user-mode monitoring without claiming kernel pre-execution blocking.
- Upgraded normal updating to be fully driven from the Updates page: default GitHub release feed, in-app check/download/hash-verify/install, ready-to-restart state, in-app rollback, and Update Service `--rollback` support.
- Current Ultracode continuation: added cooperative local-core scan cancellation with a `cancel_scan` command, ProgramData runtime cancellation token, cancelled report/progress status, Flutter graceful cancellation request, and a focused Rust regression test.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened update apply/rollback path safety with safe package IDs, safe snapshot names, canonical install/snapshot roots, symlink rejection, destination containment, and focused Rust regression tests.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened local-core quarantine integrity with regular-file checks, verified copy-then-remove fallback, source/destination hash comparison, symlink payload rejection, staged metadata writes, and focused Rust regression tests.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened local-core allowlist validation and persistence by blocking additional unsafe Windows roots such as Program Files, ProgramData, and secondary drive roots, while preserving exact hash binding for file/app/executable allowlist entries.
- Current Ultracode continuation: replaced the placeholder Allowlist screen with local-core backed list/remove management, explicit remove confirmation, app-state refresh, and backend deactivate-not-delete behavior.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened guard-service external driver scan trust boundaries so caller-provided publisher/signature/hash metadata, known-good/user-approved hashes, and protection-mode downgrades cannot bypass policy.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Guard Service guard-mode configuration path handling so `AVORAX_GUARD_MODE_CONFIG`, `ZENTOR_GUARD_MODE_CONFIG`, `AVORAX_CONFIG_DIR`, and `AVORAX_DATA_DIR` must be non-empty absolute paths instead of allowing relative working-directory policy files.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Guard Service event/fatal log base path handling so event log overrides must be non-empty absolute paths and the relative `.avorax/events` fallback is removed.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Guard Service quarantine base path handling so quarantine directory overrides must be non-empty absolute paths and the relative `.avorax/quarantine` fallback is removed.
- Current Ultracode continuation: removed the remaining Guard Service guard-mode config relative fallback, so missing ProgramData/HOME/config roots fail visibly instead of defaulting to `.avorax/config`.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Guard Service driver IPC fail-open runtime-root candidate handling so relative `SystemRoot`/ProgramData/product/quarantine/HOME environment values are ignored instead of trusted.
- Current Ultracode continuation: removed hardcoded `C:\ProgramData` and `C:\Program Files` fail-open root fallbacks from Guard Service driver IPC so product/quarantine trust roots must come from validated runtime environment candidates.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Guard Service driver-health `sc query` handling so only explicit service-absent/1060 output is treated as not installed; other nonzero probe results become visible probe failures.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Guard Service driver-health Secure Boot PowerShell probing to use checked PowerShell with UTF-16LE `-EncodedCommand` and `-NonInteractive` instead of raw `-Command`.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened local-core Core/Guard service-status `sc query` handling so only explicit service-absent/1060 output becomes `missing`/`off`; other nonzero probe results remain `unknown`.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Guard Service ClamAV compatibility scanning so the external scanner uses bounded stdout/stderr readers, a 120-second timeout, and visible kill/reap diagnostics instead of unbounded `.output()?` buffering.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Guard Service ClamAV compatibility exit-status handling so only exit 0 is treated as clean/no-match and non-1 scanner failures become visible bounded errors instead of silently returning no match.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Guard Service ClamAV infected-exit parsing so exit 1 always returns a compat detection, using a safe fallback name when scanner output lacks a parseable signature.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Guard Service compatibility scan-target validation so ClamAV and YARA helpers require non-following regular-file metadata before scanner/rule reads.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Guard driver IPC hash/YARA target handling so driver verdict hashing and YARA sample reads use non-following driver scan-candidate metadata before opening files.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Guard self-test hash target handling so protection self-test fixture hashing rejects symlink, reparse, and non-regular targets before opening files.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Guard driver IPC hash-read error reporting so unreadable local hash evidence is carried into hash, publisher, malformed-metadata, and unknown verdict reasons instead of being silently hidden behind fallback policy.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened native script analyzer dispatch so unsupported file types return a visible error instead of silently producing an all-zero `ScriptAnalysis`.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened native ML feature lookup so unknown feature names fail visibly during scoring instead of silently contributing `0.0`.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened native process-start action mapping so verdict-to-action translation is exhaustive and new verdict variants cannot silently default to `allow`.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened native risk-fusion recommended actions so scan verdict actions are mapped through an exhaustive helper rather than a wildcard `allow` fallback.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened native signature action-policy conversion so unsupported policies fail visibly instead of degrading to `Observation`.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened native rule match weighting so verdict/confidence weights are explicit instead of using a wildcard low-weight fallback.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened native threat-intel default confidence mapping so indicator types are explicit instead of using a wildcard `Confidence::Medium` fallback.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened native threat-intel indicator normalization so non-hash pass-through types are explicit instead of using a wildcard trimmed-value fallback.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened native file-walker handling so non-regular entries under scan roots increment skipped counts and emit bounded scan errors instead of being silently ignored.
- Current Ultracode Rust IPC command size-bound pass: local-core and guard-service stdin command JSON now reject payloads over 256 KiB before `serde_json` parsing. Checkpoint 1605 runtime-verifies this now that Cargo is available: `cargo test --manifest-path core\zentor_local_core\Cargo.toml oversized_json_before -- --test-threads=1` passed with `2 passed`, and `cargo test --manifest-path core\zentor_guard_service\Cargo.toml oversized_json_before -- --test-threads=1` passed with `2 passed`.
- Current Ultracode Rust IPC command size-bound pass: source marker scan confirmed `MAX_CORE_COMMAND_JSON_BYTES`, `parse_core_command_line`, `MAX_GUARD_COMMAND_JSON_BYTES`, `parse_guard_command_line`, and oversized command fixture markers.

Checkpoint 1611 runtime-verifies exact publisher matching, normalization, empty/NUL config rejection, lookalike rejection, trusted-publisher allow, and strong-malware override fixtures; each focused Cargo filter passed with `1 passed; 0 failed`.
- Current Ultracode local-core publisher exact-match pass: source marker scan confirmed `normalize_publisher_name`, exact-match publisher fixtures, the Lockdown lookalike fixture, and no old `normalized.contains(trusted)` substring trust pattern.

- Current Ultracode local-core passthrough exact-root pass: local-core application-control passthrough now normalizes path text and requires exact configured/conventional roots for Windows system directories, product quarantine roots, Unix system roots, and product service executables, so user-controlled lookalike paths no longer inherit `SystemTrusted` status. Checkpoint 1611 runtime-verifies exact system/quarantine root allow, lookalike system/quarantine rejection, service-name outside product-root rejection, and unknown unsigned Lockdown block fixtures; each focused Cargo filter passed with `1 passed; 0 failed`.

- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened guard-service quarantine creation with regular-file checks, stale-source hash rejection, verified copy fallback, destination hash validation, symlink-source rejection, staged metadata writes, and focused Rust regression tests.
- Current Ultracode continuation: added local-core quarantine metadata auth sidecars with per-store keying, tamper skipping, missing-key fail-closed behavior, staged key/tag writes, and legacy record compatibility.
- Current Ultracode continuation: added guard-service quarantine metadata auth sidecars with per-store keying, tamper detection, missing-key fail-closed behavior, staged key/tag writes, and legacy record compatibility.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened local-core and guard-service quarantine creation to reject symlink sources before metadata-following, with Unix-gated regression tests.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened local-core and guard-service quarantine creation to reject Windows reparse-point sources before metadata-following, with Windows-gated constant tests.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened local-core and guard-service quarantine base directories to reject symlink/reparse redirection before payload and metadata-key writes.
- Current Ultracode continuation: added Windows-only quarantine base ACL hardening for local-core and guard-service using fail-closed `icacls` enforcement.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened local-core and guard-service quarantine metadata auth sidecars and metadata key files against symlink/reparse redirection.
- Current Ultracode continuation: added Windows DPAPI wrapping/unwrapping for local-core and guard-service quarantine metadata-auth keys, while preserving plaintext compatibility off Windows and for legacy keys.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened local-core Recovery Vault backup/restore path integrity with regular-file checks, opaque `.avoraxrv` backup names, vault containment, symlink/reparse rejection, staged restore verification, and focused regression tests.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened local-core legacy-data migration with symlink/reparse rejection, bounded recursion, destination validation, no-overwrite behavior, staged marker/log writes, and focused regression tests.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened local-core app-control exact-hash trust stores so malformed known-good, known-bad, and user-approved hash entries fail closed.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened guard-service exact-hash trust handling so malformed known-bad cache, known-good cache, user-approved, and trusted request hashes fail closed.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened local-core guard mode and ransomware guard config writes with staged activation, link/reparse rejection, and focused regression tests.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened local-core scan cancellation token writes, checks, and cleanup with staged activation and link/reparse rejection.
- Current Ultracode continuation: bounded local-core YARA-style rule inspection to a 1 MiB sample and added a boundary regression test.
- Current Ultracode continuation: bounded local-core heuristic script and entropy checks to 1 MiB samples and added boundary regression tests.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened local-core static-feature and ClamAV/local-signature scan inputs so unreadable/non-regular files no longer receive default empty content or empty SHA-256 values; those failures flow through scan error reporting instead of fake-clean feature data.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened local YARA-style and heuristic provider inspection APIs to return `Result<Option<ThreatResult>>`, preserving the distinction between no match and failed inspection.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened local-core compatibility threat builders so signature and local-AI detections reject missing hashes and non-regular targets instead of displaying empty hashes or zero-byte fake metadata.
- Current Ultracode continuation: made local-core scan startup return an honest `EngineUnavailable` scan report when the native engine cannot initialize, with skipped counts and scan error details instead of aborting before report construction.
- Current Ultracode continuation: made auto-quarantine failures during scans visible through `scan_errors` while preserving the detection record, and added a safe-EICAR regression for an invalid quarantine destination.
- Current Ultracode continuation: made environment-backed allowlist loading fail visibly on read/JSON errors, with UI commands returning errors and scans recording an allowlist-unavailable scan error while failing closed.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Flutter allowlist refresh so local-core list failures are surfaced as UI errors and no longer collapse into an empty allowlist state.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Flutter quarantine refresh so local-core list failures are surfaced as UI errors and no longer collapse into an empty quarantine state.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Flutter Guard/ransomware config calls so local-core failures preserve exact error text instead of collapsing to a boolean.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Flutter watcher start/stop parsing so malformed or failed watcher responses surface error text instead of appearing as a clean off state.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Flutter scan completion state so reports with `scanErrors` keep a visible warning outside the scan results panel instead of clearing global errors.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Flutter scan report/progress parsing to accept both Rust camelCase and legacy snake_case fields so `scanErrors`, counters, and progress cannot silently parse as empty or zero.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Flutter threat/risk parsing to accept both camelCase and snake_case fields so detection metadata, actions, risk scores, and engines are not silently downgraded.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Flutter quarantine and allowlist parsing to accept both camelCase and snake_case fields so evidence/history records are not silently downgraded.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Flutter local-core and install-report filesystem probes so non-following executable/report checks surface inspection failures, blank environment overrides are ignored, explicit executable overrides remain visible in failure messages, and missing Core/Guard service executables report the inspected path.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Flutter scan routing so a failed single-target file-vs-path probe produces a failed `ScanReport` with `scanErrors` instead of silently falling back to `scanPaths`.
- Current Ultracode continuation: hardened Flutter update-service executable probes so required updater path failures include filesystem inspection diagnostics and discovery no longer uses the old boolean-only candidate helper.
- Current Ultracode continuation: bounded Flutter update diagnostics so update check, install, rollback, and package-cleanup failure details no longer place unbounded exception text into UI state, audit details, or combined cleanup errors.
- Current Ultracode continuation: bounded Flutter local-core action diagnostics for quarantine, feedback, allowlist, restore, and delete failures before UI/audit reporting.
- Current Ultracode continuation: bounded Flutter protection/privilege action diagnostics for Core Service start, install-report open, repair, protection start/stop, and self-test failures.
- Current Ultracode continuation: bounded Flutter protection-profile and ransomware-guard settings diagnostics, including rollback failure details, before UI/audit reporting.
- Current Ultracode continuation: bounded Flutter scan/settings operation diagnostics for scheduled scan settings, custom scan pickers, scan cancellation, scan invocation failures, log export, and configuration reset.
- Current Ultracode continuation: completed app-state controller diagnostic bounding so raw `'$error'` writes are removed outside the shared bounded helper.
- Current Ultracode continuation: bounded Flutter update-service diagnostics for installed-version discovery, GitHub release-feed fallback, and updater file-probe inspection failures.
- Current Ultracode continuation: bounded Flutter LocalCoreClient exception diagnostics for self-test, install-report open, IPC call failures, cancel IPC, and executable file probes.

## Blockers

- Superseded by checkpoint 1547: update-service test binaries now execute in the non-elevated shell, and `cargo test -p avorax_update_service -- --test-threads=1` passes with `176 passed`.
- Windows release and protection gates still require a driver validation/self-test report at `dist\windows-driver-validation\selftest_report.json`; this environment has not built, installed, run, or self-tested the signed driver path.
- Superseded by checkpoints 1546 and 1548: Flutter/Dart are available on this host; `flutter test --reporter compact` plus `flutter analyze` pass for `apps/zentor_client`; and `packages/zentor_protocol` now passes `dart format --set-exit-if-changed lib test`, `dart analyze`, and `dart test --reporter expanded`.
- No production update signing key is configured here. The package builder intentionally refuses unsigned `.aup` output.
- No signed Windows driver has been built, installed, run, or self-tested in this environment.
- Superseded by checkpoints 1545 and 1547: native-engine Rust tests now pass on this host (`cargo test -p zentor_native_engine --lib -- --test-threads=1`, `284 passed`), and full `cargo test --workspace -- --test-threads=1` passes.

- Current Ultracode update path-safety consolidation pass: static shared path-safety marker check passed.
- Current Ultracode scan error-detail pass: walker and native scan errors are now bounded in `scan_errors`, skipped files are not reported clean, and the Flutter scan results UI displays completed-with-errors diagnostics.
- Current Ultracode local-core action error-propagation pass: quarantine, restore, delete, allowlist, and detection-label failures now preserve local-core IPC error details in UI state instead of reducing them to generic failure messages.
- Current Ultracode Flutter update-cache staging pass: in-app `.aup` downloads now write to a temporary `.part` file, verify SHA-256 before cache activation, reject linked cache paths, and replace only regular cached files.
- Current Ultracode Flutter update-cache pre-create pass: existing linked cache directories are rejected before creation/use, temporary package names are checked before writes, and local `file:` package sources must be regular non-linked files.
- Current Ultracode update-service checked-create pass: staging, install, rollback, and payload-copy directory creation now rejects linked/reparse ancestors before and after `create_dir_all`.
- Current Ultracode update-service staged-file pass: update payload and rollback file copies now write synced `.avorax-part` siblings before activation and recheck linked/reparse targets.
- Current Ultracode update-service staged-copy cleanup pass: staged `.avorax-part` files are removed when post-write activation fails before the original error is returned.
- Current Ultracode update-service staged-temp allocation pass: staged copy temp names now include process/time/attempt components, reject linked/reparse candidates, and tolerate stale regular `.avorax-part` siblings.
- Current Ultracode update extraction temp allocation pass: payload extraction temp names now include process/time/attempt components, destination directories use checked creation, and stale regular `.avorax-part` siblings no longer block extraction.
- Current Ultracode update report log hardening pass: update report log names are basename-restricted and report writes use checked staged byte writes under the update log directory.
- Current Ultracode update package path-validation pass: native update verify/apply now requires regular non-linked `.aup` package paths before parsing, hashing, payload verification, or extraction.
- Current Ultracode rollback snapshot selection pass: latest rollback discovery now rejects linked/reparse rollback roots and ignores unsafe snapshot directory names.
- Current Ultracode rollback snapshot completeness pass: snapshot creation and restore now require the app executable, core service executable, guard service executable, and engine directory, failing closed on missing, linked, reparse-backed, or wrong-type rollback components.
- Current Ultracode update-service restart fail-safe pass: Windows `sc.exe` stop/start failures now propagate, failed rollback restarts are recorded, and service restart failure after payload replacement triggers rollback plus a failed update report.
- Current Ultracode update manifest size-limit pass: `.aup` `manifest.json` reads are capped at 1 MiB and `manifest.sig` reads are capped at 16 KiB before parsing or signature verification.
- Current Ultracode update driver-payload rejection pass: native `.aup` verification, payload limit validation, and extraction now reject `payload/driver` and `payload/driver-tools` roots at runtime.
- Current Ultracode update component/payload consistency pass: native `.aup` verification now rejects payload roots that are unknown or not declared by the signed manifest components.
- Current Ultracode update subcomponent/payload consistency pass: native `.aup` verification now requires service filenames and engine subdirectories to match their specific signed manifest component flags.
- Current Ultracode update manifest operational-flag pass: normal `.aup` verification now rejects unsupported self-update, driver-tools, no-admin, no-restart, and reboot-required update modes.
- Current Ultracode update manifest string-validation pass: native `.aup` verification now bounds and shape-validates versions, package IDs, public-key IDs, release dates, release notes URLs, and migration step names.
- Current Ultracode update manifest version-validation pass: native `.aup` verification now requires `version` and `previous_min_version` to be dotted numeric strings that match comparator semantics.
- Current Ultracode update policy current-version pass: native `.aup` verification now rejects malformed installed/current policy versions before comparing update order.
- Current Ultracode update release-notes HTTPS pass: native `.aup` verification now rejects plain-HTTP release notes URLs in signed manifests.

## Exact Next Task

Continue the highest-value source-level path-safety hardening that can be verified without Cargo, then validate quarantine, Recovery Vault, migration, guard, and native-engine changes on a provisioned Windows Rust host.

## Final Limitations

Avorax must not claim kernel-level or pre-execution protection until the signed driver path is built, installed, running, and self-tested. In-app updates do not silently update drivers; driver updates require a separate explicit driver workflow.

- Current Ultracode Windows installer build hardening pass: `installer\windows\build-msi.ps1` now requires explicit checked `-DotnetPath`, `-CargoPath`, and `-FlutterPath`/environment tool paths, rejects unsafe installer version/configuration tokens, removes ambient build-tool lookup, writes generated configs/manifests/WiX/license/report/helper files through checked atomic staging, gates optional ClamAV downloads behind `-AllowClamAVDownload`, verifies pinned SHA-256 before cache activation, safely extracts zip entries with traversal/absolute/duplicate/size/count checks, and generates a driver install helper/custom action that avoid ambient PowerShell/System32 tool lookup.
- Current Ultracode Windows installer build hardening pass: checks `checkpoint 671 build-msi parse check passed` and `checkpoint 671 build-msi source check passed`; negative sanity checks confirmed `-Version '..\bad'` fails with `Installer version contains unsafe characters: ..\bad` and a build without explicit `-DotnetPath` fails with `.NET SDK dotnet executable path is required. Refusing to launch an ambient command from PATH.`
- Current Ultracode Windows installer build hardening pass: full MSI/EXE build was not executed because this shell does not have explicit validated `dotnet`, `cargo`, and `flutter` release-host paths or a verified packaged Windows stage fixture; the generated driver install helper was not executed because it can import certificates, inspect TESTSIGNING, install drivers, and load a minifilter in an elevated Windows environment.
- Current Ultracode Windows protection self-test Cargo-path pass: `tools\windows\zentor-protection-selftest.ps1` now resolves the repository root from the script location, requires explicit checked `-CargoPath`/`CARGO`, validates build/install/self-test scripts and repo-contained report paths as local non-reparse paths, builds Guard Service through `& $cargo build --release`, validates the built Guard Service executable, and passes it explicitly to the minifilter self-test through `-GuardServicePath`.
- Current Ultracode Windows protection self-test Cargo-path pass: checks `checkpoint 672 protection selftest parse check passed`, `checkpoint 672 protection selftest source check passed`, and `checkpoint 672 protection selftest no-ambient-cargo sanity check passed`; documentation check confirmed no README/docs/TESTING/AGENTS protection self-test command remains without `-CargoPath`.
- Current Ultracode Windows protection self-test Cargo-path pass: full protection self-test was not executed because this shell lacks a validated Cargo toolchain, a built Guard Service, and an approved elevated Windows driver-development VM; driver build/install scripts were not executed because they can build, install, start, stop, or load Windows drivers.
- Current Ultracode live driver-remediation staging pass: `tools\windows\avorax-fix-driver-live.ps1` now validates the ProgramData report directory/log paths, writes `latest.json` atomically, writes the generated post-reboot SYSTEM script atomically, validates install-helper candidates as regular non-reparse files, and generates a post-reboot script that revalidates embedded `bcdedit.exe`, `fltmc.exe`, `sc.exe`, PowerShell, and optional install-helper paths before launch while atomically writing `post-reboot.json`.

- Current Ultracode Windows desktop build-readiness recheck: checkpoint 1935 refreshes the current host blockers for a real Windows `Avorax.exe`/installer path. `flutter doctor -v` passes Flutter/Windows device checks but reports missing Android SDK and missing Visual Studio Desktop C++ components; the Android SDK remains out of scope for the Windows antivirus release path, but the Visual Studio C++ workload is a Windows desktop blocker. Host-only `tools\windows\avorax-release-prereq-check.ps1` writes `dist\release-prereq\host_only_1935.json` with `ok=false`, 24 checks, and exactly three real host errors: explicit .NET SDK path missing/no SDK inventory, unavailable Windows symlink support, and missing Visual Studio Desktop C++ components. Direct `flutter build windows --debug --no-pub` fails immediately with Flutter's symlink-support/Developer Mode prerequisite, and `dotnet --list-sdks` confirms `No SDKs were found` for `C:\Program Files\dotnet\dotnet.exe`. Evidence is recorded in `.workflow\ultracode\avorax-hardening\results\1935-windows-desktop-build-readiness.md`. This keeps Windows desktop artifact/installer E2E blocked honestly; it does not change the passing small-threat MVP verifier or claim an installed product build.
- Current Ultracode repair-installation privilege-boundary pass: checkpoint 1936 hardens the Flutter local-core service repair action so it no longer reuses development `target\release` local-core candidates for Windows service registration. Scans, self-tests, and cancel IPC can still use development executables for local validation, but `repairInstallation()` now resolves only installed/explicit service executables, canonicalizes the checked executable path before policy checks, and refuses to register a development checkout executable as the Windows Core Service. Runtime coverage proves a repo `target\release\zentor_local_core.exe` is rejected before any elevated PowerShell launch, source markers prove the installed-only resolver excludes `_developmentExecutableCandidates(...)`, full `local_core_ipc_diagnostics_test.dart` passes (`66`), focused service action tests pass (`4`), Flutter analyzer passes, source-contracts pass (`512`), and the expanded small-threat MVP verifier passes end-to-end in `86.9s`. Evidence is recorded in `.workflow\ultracode\avorax-hardening\results\1936-repair-installation-service-boundary.md`. This is a privilege-boundary hardening for visible repair controls; installed service/UI E2E and real installer repair remain blocked until a Windows release host can build and install Avorax.
- Current Ultracode small-threat verifier repair-boundary coverage pass: checkpoint 1937 adds `Flutter repair-installation boundary tests` to `tools\testing\verify-small-threat-mvp.ps1`, so the one-command MVP sweep now proves the visible repair control still refuses development checkout service registration and keeps installed service repair E2E classified as partial. Source-contracts now assert the verifier step, repair-boundary scope text, and installed service repair limitation. Evidence is recorded in `.workflow\ultracode\avorax-hardening\results\1937-small-threat-verifier-repair-boundary.md`. This is verifier coverage hardening only; it does not prove installed service repair E2E, installed UI/service operation, machine-wide service changes, signed-driver behavior, or pre-execution blocking.

- Current Ultracode small-threat verifier Defender-noise reduction pass: checkpoint 1939 changes the default `tools\testing\verify-small-threat-mvp.ps1` run so it no longer creates the standard EICAR file that Microsoft Defender reports as `DOS/EICAR_Test_File`. - Current Ultracode protection start failure-honesty pass: checkpoint 1940 prevents the Flutter controller from reporting or persisting realtime protection as started when Guard mode configuration fails and the best-effort watcher is also inactive. In that no-active-local-layer case, `startProtection()` now logs `protection_start_failed`, sets `ProtectionStatus.error`, preserves `realtimeProtectionEnabled=false` for a fresh start, and surfaces bounded Guard/watcher diagnostics instead of `ProtectionStatus.partiallyProtected`. The fake local-core test helper now supports ordered Guard mode results so start-success/stop-failure flows remain precisely testable, and `tools\testing\verify-small-threat-mvp.ps1` now includes `Flutter protection start-stop controller tests` in the one-command sweep.
